skill.name: flux-2-klein
skill.description: Generate images with Flux 2 Klein (Black Forest Labs' distilled fast variant of Flux 2) on RunComfy — bundled with the model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper output than naive prompting against the same model. Documents Flux 2 Klein's strengths (sub-second latency, multi-reference brand styling, declarative subject-first prompts), the step-count strategy (4–8 for fast iteration, ~25 for polish), the 9B vs 4B variant trade-off, and when to route to Flux 2 Pro / Seedream 5 / GPT Image 2 instead. Calls `runcomfy run blackforestlabs/flux-2-klein/9b/text-to-image` (or `/4b/`) through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "flux 2 klein", "flux-2-klein", "flux klein", "BFL flux 2", or any explicit ask to generate with this model.
skill.summary: Generate images with Flux 2 Klein (Black Forest Labs' distilled fast variant of Flux 2) on RunComfy — bundled with the model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper output than naive prompting against the same model.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: AI review of 83 static findings across 1 file (224 lines). All 54 external_commands findings are false positives — backtick-enclosed bash snippets in markdown are documentation code blocks, not Ruby shell execution. All 5 filesystem findings are false positives — path references describe CLI behavior documentation (polling endpoints, token storage), not traversal. All 8 network findings point to legitimate RunComfy service URLs required for the skill's function. All 16 blocker-level findings (10 weak crypto, 5 system recon, 1 at command) are misidentified markdown documentation text, not actual code. The skill is a documentation-only markdown file that instructs the AI on how to use the RunComfy CLI for image generation. No actual code execution, no command injection surface (CLI uses JSON input via --input flag), and no telemetry or callbacks. Confirmed safe for publication.

content.user_title: Generate Images with Flux 2 Klein on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Getting high-quality AI-generated images often requires complex setup and slow iteration cycles. This skill connects you to Black Forest Labs' Flux 2 Klein through the RunComfy CLI for sub-second image generation with optimized prompting patterns.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using Flux 2 Klein 9B and 4B models through the RunComfy CLI
Use subject-first declarative prompts for sharper and more predictable image output
Control image dimensions, step count, and aspect ratio for precise output tuning
Apply multi-reference image style transfer with up to 4 reference images
Route between Flux 2 Klein, Flux 2 Pro, Seedream 5, and GPT Image 2 based on quality needs
limitations: Requires a working RunComfy account and CLI installation before use
Maximum resolution is approximately 2K with aspect ratio capped at 16:9
Prompt length is limited to approximately 512 tokens before quality degrades
Up to 4 reference images supported; additional images increase latency

use_cases.title: Rapid Creative Iteration
use_cases.description: Generate image concepts in real-time using the 4B variant for sub-second feedback during live art direction sessions.

use_cases.title: Polished Final Output
use_cases.description: Produce high-quality final images using the 9B variant with 25 steps for detailed and cinematic results.

use_cases.title: Brand-Consistent Asset Packs
use_cases.description: Create unified visual asset packs using multi-reference style transfer to maintain consistent aesthetics across all outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a cat sitting on a windowsill, soft natural light, realistic style.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a simple image

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Product Photography
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a product photo of a ceramic coffee mug on a wooden table, softbox lighting from the left side, shallow depth of field, neutral background, e-commerce ready style.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating marketing assets for an online store

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reference Brand Styling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the same composition and lighting as the reference images, but change the product color to blue with white typography. Keep the product silhouette and background elements exactly as in the references.
prompt_templates.scenario: Maintaining brand consistency across a product line

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic Scene with Technical Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a cinematic landscape at 1536x864 resolution using the 9B variant with 25 steps. A lone figure walking on a misty mountain ridge at sunrise, warm golden light, volumetric fog, anamorphic lens style, epic scale.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a high-quality cinematic scene for a presentation

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a modern minimalist living room with large windows, natural light, neutral colors, plants in the corners.
output_examples.output: Created a 1024x1024 image showing a bright living room with floor-to-ceiling windows, beige sofas, a wooden coffee table, and potted monstera plants in the corners. The lighting is warm and natural with soft shadows.

output_examples.input: Generate a product photo of a matte black smartphone on a marble surface, studio lighting, shallow depth of field.
output_examples.output: Created a 1024x1024 e-commerce image showing a black smartphone centered on white marble. Studio lighting creates soft reflections on the polished surface. The background fades into a soft gradient blur.

best_practices: Use subject-first declarative prompts: start with the main subject, then describe action, scene, style, lighting, and quality markers.
Match step count to your phase: use 4 to 8 steps on 4B for fast concepting, then 25 steps on 9B for final polish.
Keep reference images in the same visual style when using multi-reference mode for best alignment results.
anti_patterns: Do not use conflicting adjectives such as minimalist plus ornate in the same prompt as they cancel each other out.
Do not exceed approximately 512 tokens as model quality degrades instead of truncating gracefully.
Do not request ultra-wide aspect ratios beyond 16:9 as the model crops the result instead of adapting.

faq.question: What is Flux 2 Klein?
faq.answer: Flux 2 Klein is a distilled fast variant of Flux 2 by Black Forest Labs. It offers sub-second latency for rapid image generation iteration.

faq.question: Do I need a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a RunComfy account and the RunComfy CLI installed. Run npm i -g @runcomfy/cli and runcomfy login to set up.

faq.question: What is the difference between 4B and 9B variants?
faq.answer: The 4B variant is optimized for speed with sub-second inference for concepting. The 9B variant provides higher quality output for final polished images.

faq.question: What image sizes are supported?
faq.answer: Typical dimensions range from 512 to 1536 pixels. The aspect ratio is capped at 16:9 and maximum resolution is approximately 2K.

faq.question: Can I use reference images?
faq.answer: Yes, up to 4 reference images are supported for style transfer and guided composition on the same endpoint.

faq.question: How many steps should I use?
faq.answer: Use 4 to 8 steps for fast concepting on the 4B variant. Use approximately 25 steps for final polish on the 9B variant. More than 25 steps adds little improvement.
